title: armeria glaberrima
intro: Armeria glaberrima, also known as the small-flowered thrift or sea pink, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Armeria glaberrima are believed to provide health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing swelling, and supporting the immune system. Armeria glaberrima is typically prepared as an infusion, tincture, or topical ointment, and is often used to treat minor wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
